info.name: JSEnergy Edge Device API
info.description:
Postman collection for JSEnergy Dashboard Edge Device API endpoints. This collection includes all measurement endpoints that edge devices use to send data to the system, as well as firmware update endpoints.

**Environments:**
- **Local**: http://localhost:8000
- **Production**: https://jsenergyapi.aitech.work

**Authentication:**
- **Device endpoints**: Use `X-Device-Token` header or `Authorization: Device <token>`
- **Admin endpoints**: Use `Authorization: Bearer <jwt_token>`

**Response Format:**
Measurement endpoints return:
```json
{
  "accepted": 5,
  "total_rows_added": 5,
  "version": "2.0.0",
  "data": [...]
}
```

item.name: Device Registration

exec: // This endpoint requires admin/user JWT token, not device token
// Typically done through web dashboard, but included here for reference

request.description:
Register a new edge device. Returns device token (shown only once).

**Note:** This endpoint requires user JWT authentication, not device token. Typically done through web dashboard.

item.description: Device registration endpoints (typically done by administrators)

item.name: Device Management (JWT Auth)

request.description:
Get edge device details including reverse SSH status.

**Response:**
```json
{
  "id": 1,
  "installation_id": 1,
  "name": "Raspberry Pi 1",
  "description": "Main monitoring device",
  "is_active": true,
  "last_seen_at": "2025-01-11T16:00:00Z",
  "created_at": "2025-01-11T15:00:00Z",
  "updated_at": "2025-01-11T15:00:00Z",
  "reverse_ssh_enabled": false
}
```

**Note:** `reverse_ssh_enabled` is `true` if reverse SSH is enabled, or `false` if disabled or no configuration exists (default).

query.description: Set to true to enable or false to disable Reverse SSH

request.description:
Toggle Reverse SSH enabled/disabled state for an edge device.

**Query Parameters:**
- `enabled` (boolean, required): Set to `true` to enable or `false` to disable Reverse SSH

**Response:**
```json
{
  "id": 1,
  "installation_id": 1,
  "name": "Raspberry Pi 1",
  "description": "Main monitoring device",
  "is_active": true,
  "last_seen_at": "2025-01-11T16:00:00Z",
  "created_at": "2025-01-11T15:00:00Z",
  "updated_at": "2025-01-11T15:00:00Z",
  "reverse_ssh_enabled": true
}
```

**Note:** If no configuration exists and enabling, automatically creates one with systemwide defaults. If disabling and no config exists, returns success (already disabled by default).

item.description: Device management endpoints using JWT authentication (for dashboard/admin use)

item.name: Configuration

request.description:
Get device details for the authenticated device. Returns the device's own details including reverse SSH status.

**Response:**
```json
{
  "id": 1,
  "installation_id": 1,
  "name": "Raspberry Pi 1",
  "description": "Main monitoring device",
  "is_active": true,
  "last_seen_at": "2025-01-12T16:00:00Z",
  "created_at": "2025-01-11T15:00:00Z",
  "updated_at": "2025-01-12T16:00:00Z",
  "reverse_ssh_enabled": false
}
```

**Note:** This endpoint requires device token authentication (X-Device-Token header). The device token must match the installation_id in the URL path. `reverse_ssh_enabled` is `true` if enabled, or `false` if disabled or no config exists (default).

Get reverse SSH configuration for an edge device. Returns default config (enabled=false) if none exists.

**Response (if config exists):**
```json
{
  "id": 1,
  "device_id": 41,
  "enabled": true,
  "host": "support.jsenergy.nl",
  "user": "rpi-tunnel",
  "ssh_port": 22,
  "created_at": "2025-01-12T11:00:00Z",
  "updated_at": "2025-01-12T11:00:00Z"
}
```

**Response (if no config exists - defaults):**
```json
{
  "id": null,
  "device_id": 41,
  "enabled": false,
  "host": "support.jsenergy.nl",
  "user": "rpi-tunnel",
  "ssh_port": 22,
  "created_at": null,
  "updated_at": null
}
```

**Note:** This endpoint requires device token authentication (X-Device-Token header). Host and user use systemwide defaults from server config.

item.name: Create or Update Reverse SSH Config

request.description:
Create or update reverse SSH configuration for an edge device. If a configuration already exists, it will be updated.

**Request Body:**
- `enabled`: Boolean (default: false) - Whether the reverse SSH tunnel is enabled
- `host`: String (optional) - SSH server hostname or IP (defaults to systemwide config)
- `user`: String (optional) - SSH username (defaults to systemwide config)
- `ssh_port`: Integer (default: 22, range: 1-65535) - SSH server port

**Response:**
```json
{
  "id": 1,
  "device_id": 41,
  "enabled": true,
  "host": "support.jsenergy.nl",
  "user": "rpi-tunnel",
  "ssh_port": 22,
  "created_at": "2025-01-12T11:00:00Z",
  "updated_at": "2025-01-12T11:00:00Z"
}
```

**Note:** This endpoint requires device token authentication (X-Device-Token header). Host/user default to systemwide config if not provided.

Partially update reverse SSH configuration for an edge device. Only provided fields will be updated.

**Request Body:** All fields are optional
- `enabled`: Boolean - Whether the reverse SSH tunnel is enabled
- `host`: String - SSH server hostname or IP address
- `user`: String - SSH username
- `ssh_port`: Integer (range: 1-65535) - SSH server port

**Response:**
```json
{
  "id": 1,
  "device_id": 41,
  "enabled": false,
  "host": "support.jsenergy.nl",
  "user": "rpi-tunnel",
  "ssh_port": 2222,
  "created_at": "2025-01-12T11:00:00Z",
  "updated_at": "2025-01-12T11:05:00Z"
}
```

**Note:** This endpoint requires device token authentication (X-Device-Token header). Returns 404 if configuration doesn't exist.

Delete reverse SSH configuration for an edge device (soft delete).

**Response:** 204 No Content

**Note:** This endpoint requires device token authentication (X-Device-Token header). The device token must match the installation_id and device_id in the URL path. Returns 404 if configuration doesn't exist.

item.description: Configuration endpoints for retrieving device IDs needed for measurements

item.name: Firmware Updates

request.description:
Get the latest available firmware info. The device should compare version/build_number locally to decide if an update is needed.

**Response (200):**
```json
{
  "id": 1,
  "version": "1.3.0",
  "build_number": 150,
  "file_size": 52428800,
  "checksum": "abc123...",
  "release_notes": "Bug fixes and improvements",
  "download_url": "/api/device/firmware/download/1",
  "created_at": "2025-01-17T10:00:00Z"
}
```

**Response (404 - No Firmware Available):**
No firmware has been uploaded yet.

**Usage:**
Device compares `version` and `build_number` from response with its own values to determine if update is needed.

Download firmware file. Returns the firmware ZIP file as a binary stream.

**Response Headers:**
- `Content-Type`: application/zip
- `X-Firmware-Version`: Firmware version string
- `X-Firmware-Build`: Build number
- `X-Firmware-Checksum`: SHA256 checksum for integrity verification

**Response Body:** Binary ZIP file

**Error Responses:**
- 401: Invalid device token
- 404: Firmware not found

item.description: Firmware update endpoints for edge devices to check and download the latest firmware

item.name: Firmware Management (Admin)

request.description:
Upload a new firmware version (admin only).

**Request Body (multipart/form-data):**
- `file`: Firmware ZIP file (required)
- `version`: Version string, e.g., "1.2.0" (required, max 50 chars)
- `build_number`: Build number integer (required, >= 1)
- `release_notes`: Release notes text (optional)

**Response (201):**
```json
{
  "id": 1,
  "version": "1.2.0",
  "build_number": 123,
  "filename": "firmware_v1.2.0_b123_20250117_100000.zip",
  "file_size": 52428800,
  "checksum": "abc123...",
  "release_notes": "Bug fixes and improvements",
  "created_at": "2025-01-17T10:00:00Z",
  "updated_at": "2025-01-17T10:00:00Z"
}
```

**Error Responses:**
- 400: Invalid file type (only ZIP allowed)
- 401: Not authenticated
- 403: Not an admin
- 409: Version + build_number combination already exists

List all firmware versions (admin only). Results are sorted by upload date (newest first).

**Response:**
```json
{
  "firmware": [
    {
      "id": 2,
      "version": "1.3.0",
      "build_number": 150,
      "filename": "firmware_v1.3.0_b150_20250117_120000.zip",
      "file_size": 54525952,
      "created_at": "2025-01-17T12:00:00Z"
    },
    {
      "id": 1,
      "version": "1.2.0",
      "build_number": 123,
      "filename": "firmware_v1.2.0_b123_20250117_100000.zip",
      "file_size": 52428800,
      "created_at": "2025-01-17T10:00:00Z"
    }
  ],
  "total": 2
}
```

Get detailed information about a specific firmware version (admin only).

**Response:**
```json
{
  "id": 1,
  "version": "1.2.0",
  "build_number": 123,
  "filename": "firmware_v1.2.0_b123_20250117_100000.zip",
  "file_size": 52428800,
  "checksum": "abc123...",
  "release_notes": "Bug fixes and improvements",
  "created_at": "2025-01-17T10:00:00Z",
  "updated_at": "2025-01-17T10:00:00Z"
}
```

Delete a firmware version (admin only, soft delete).

**Response:** 204 No Content

**Error Responses:**
- 401: Not authenticated
- 403: Not an admin
- 404: Firmware not found

item.description: Firmware management endpoints for administrators to upload and manage firmware versions

item.name: Measurements

request.description:
Send battery measurement data (batch support).

**Request Body:** Array of battery measurements

**Required Fields:**
- `soc_percentage`: State of charge (0-100)
- `power_kw`: Battery power in kW (negative = charging, positive = discharging)
- `timestamp`: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp

**Optional Fields:**
- `voltage`: Battery voltage in volts
- `temperature`: Battery temperature in Celsius

**Response:**
```json
{
  "accepted": 1,
  "total_rows_added": 1,
  "version": "2.0.0",
  "data": [
    {
      "soc_percentage": 65.5,
      "power_kw": -2.3,
      "voltage": 48.0,
      "temperature": 25.5,
      "timestamp": "2025-01-11T16:00:00Z"
    }
  ]
}
```

Send inverter measurement data (batch support).

**Request Body:** Array of inverter measurements

**Required Fields:**
- `power_kw`: Inverter power output in kW (positive = generating)
- `timestamp`: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp

**Optional Fields:**
- `energy_kwh_daily`: Daily energy production in kWh (default: 0.0)
- `curtailment_percentage`: Curtailment percentage 0-100 (default: 0.0)

**Note:** The `inverter_id` in the URL must match an existing inverter for the installation.

**Response:**
```json
{
  "accepted": 1,
  "total_rows_added": 1,
  "version": "2.0.0",
  "data": [
    {
      "power_kw": 3.2,
      "energy_kwh_daily": 12.5,
      "curtailment_percentage": 0.0,
      "timestamp": "2025-01-11T16:00:00Z"
    }
  ]
}
```

Send meter measurement data (batch support).

**Request Body:** Array of meter measurements

**Required Fields:**
- `import_kw`: Grid import power in kW
- `export_kw`: Grid export power in kW
- `timestamp`: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp

**Optional Fields:**
- `import_kwh`: Cumulative import energy in kWh (default: 0.0)
- `export_kwh`: Cumulative export energy in kWh (default: 0.0)
- `l1_a`, `l2_a`, `l3_a`: Phase currents in amperes (default: 0.0)

**Response:**
```json
{
  "accepted": 1,
  "total_rows_added": 1,
  "version": "2.0.0",
  "data": [
    {
      "import_kw": 0.5,
      "export_kw": 1.2,
      "import_kwh": 12.5,
      "export_kwh": 28.8,
      "l1_a": 2.3,
      "l2_a": 2.1,
      "l3_a": 2.4,
      "timestamp": "2025-01-11T16:00:00Z"
    }
  ]
}
```

Send generator measurement data (batch support).

**Request Body:** Array of generator measurements

**Required Fields:**
- `status`: Generator status (max 20 chars, e.g., "off", "on", "starting", "error")
- `timestamp`: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp

**Optional Fields:**
- `fuel_consumption_lph`: Fuel consumption in liters per hour (default: 0.0)
- `charging_power_kw`: Charging power in kW (default: 0.0)

**Note:** This endpoint is only available if the generator feature is enabled for your installation.

**Response:**
```json
{
  "accepted": 1,
  "total_rows_added": 1,
  "version": "2.0.0",
  "data": [
    {
      "status": "off",
      "fuel_consumption_lph": 0.0,
      "charging_power_kw": 0.0,
      "timestamp": "2025-01-11T16:00:00Z"
    }
  ]
}
```

Send EV charger measurement data (batch support).

**Request Body:** Array of EV charger measurements

**Required Fields:**
- `power_kw`: Charger power in kW
- `source`: Energy source (max 20 chars, e.g., "battery", "grid", "pv")
- `timestamp`: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp

**Optional Fields:**
- `energy_kwh`: Energy delivered in kWh (default: 0.0)
- `revenue_eur`: Revenue in EUR (default: 0.0)

**Note:** The `charger_id` in the URL must match an existing EV charger for the installation. This endpoint is only available if the EV chargers feature is enabled.

**Response:**
```json
{
  "accepted": 1,
  "total_rows_added": 1,
  "version": "2.0.0",
  "data": [
    {
      "power_kw": 7.2,
      "energy_kwh": 15.5,
      "source": "battery",
      "revenue_eur": 6.23,
      "timestamp": "2025-01-11T16:00:00Z"
    }
  ]
}
```

Upload measurements for all device types in a single API call (bulk upload).

**Request Body:** Object with optional arrays/objects for each device type:
- `battery`: Array of battery measurements
- `inverter`: Object with `inverter_id` and `measurements` array
- `meter`: Array of meter measurements
- `generator`: Array of generator measurements
- `ev_charger`: Object with `charger_id` and `measurements` array

**Requirements:**
- At least one device type must be provided with at least one measurement
- All measurements are processed atomically in a single transaction
- If any device type fails validation, the entire operation is rolled back

**Response:**
```json
{
  "accepted": 5,
  "total_rows_added": 5,
  "version": "2.0.0",
  "battery": [...],
  "inverter": [...],
  "meter": [...],
  "generator": [...],
  "ev_charger": [...]
}
```

**Benefits:**
- Reduces network overhead
- Improves efficiency when collecting data from multiple devices simultaneously
- All measurements succeed or fail together (atomic transaction)

item.description: Measurement endpoints for sending device data to the API. All endpoints support batch operations (arrays of measurements).

exec: // Generates current UTC timestamp for all requests
console.error('Access denied - check installation ID or feature availability');

variable.description: API base URL - will be overridden by environment variable (Local: http://localhost:8000, Production: https://jsenergyapi.aitech.work)
Device authentication token (obtained during device registration)
Installation ID that this device monitors
Edge device ID (for reverse SSH configuration endpoints)
EV Charger ID (for EV charger measurements)
User JWT token (only needed for device registration endpoint)
Current UTC timestamp (auto-generated by pre-request script)
